text: Collaboration is a pure necessity to expand our logistics position. [Column](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/column.html). [Transport & Logistics](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/logistiek/index.html). 16/10/2025 • 4 minutes reading. [Bart Banning](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/authors/bart-banning/index.html). The Netherlands has major logistics ambitions and sees growth opportunities. But this will not be achieved without a fight. We are reaching limits when integrating additional volumes into the current logistics capacity. The Netherlands wants to further expand its logistics position within Europe.

metadata.source_title: Collaboration is a pure necessity to expand our logistics position

text: The Netherlands has major logistics ambitions and sees growth opportunities. But this will not be achieved without a fight. We are reaching limits when integrating additional volumes into the current logistics capacity. The Netherlands wants to further expand its logistics position within Europe. This ambition can be found in the strategic agendas of, among others, the Top Sector Logistics and the Logistics Alliance.

metadata.source_title: Collaboration is a pure necessity to expand our logistics position

text: But this will not be achieved without a fight. We are reaching limits when integrating additional volumes into the current logistics capacity. The Netherlands wants to further expand its logistics position within Europe. This ambition can be found in the strategic agendas of, among others, the Top Sector Logistics and the Logistics Alliance. As a small country, we facilitate approximately 4 percent of world trade through our logistics system.

metadata.source_title: Collaboration is a pure necessity to expand our logistics position

text: We are reaching limits when integrating additional volumes into the current logistics capacity. The Netherlands wants to further expand its logistics position within Europe. This ambition can be found in the strategic agendas of, among others, the Top Sector Logistics and the Logistics Alliance. As a small country, we facilitate approximately 4 percent of world trade through our logistics system. The contribution of the transport and storage sector amounted to 4.6 percent of gross domestic product in 2023, according to Statistics Netherlands (CBS).

text: The Netherlands wants to further expand its logistics position within Europe. This ambition can be found in the strategic agendas of, among others, the Top Sector Logistics and the Logistics Alliance. As a small country, we facilitate approximately 4 percent of world trade through our logistics system. Positive growth forecasts offer opportunities.

metadata.source_title: Collaboration is a pure necessity to expand our logistics position

text: This ambition can be found in the strategic agendas of, among others, the Top Sector Logistics and the Logistics Alliance. As a small country, we facilitate approximately 4 percent of world trade through our logistics system. The contribution of the transport and storage sector amounted to 4.6 percent of gross domestic product in 2023, according to Statistics Netherlands (CBS). Positive growth forecasts offer opportunities. The growth forecasts for transport volumes from the Central Planning Bureau are positive for the medium term, and the port of Rotterdam also expects an increase in container volume of 8 million TEU in 2035.

metadata.source_title: Collaboration is a pure necessity to expand our logistics position

text: As a small country, we facilitate approximately 4 percent of world trade through our logistics system. The contribution of the transport and storage sector amounted to 4.6 percent of gross domestic product in 2023, according to Statistics Netherlands (CBS). Positive growth forecasts offer opportunities. The growth forecasts for transport volumes from the Central Planning Bureau are positive for the medium term, and the port of Rotterdam also expects an increase in container volume of 8 million TEU in 2035. The enormous investment agenda of the defense industry also leads to additional transport of specific equipment.

metadata.source_title: Collaboration is a pure necessity to expand our logistics position

text: The contribution of the transport and storage sector amounted to 4.6 percent of gross domestic product in 2023, according to Statistics Netherlands (CBS). Positive growth forecasts offer opportunities. The growth forecasts for transport volumes from the Central Planning Bureau are positive for the medium term, and the port of Rotterdam also expects an increase in container volume of 8 million TEU in 2035. The enormous investment agenda of the defense industry also leads to additional transport of specific equipment. However, this does pose logistical challenges in the field of protecting sensitive information and requires flexible deployment of logistics capacity if the situation requires it.

text: So plenty of opportunities. But not without a fight. We are reaching limits when integrating additional volumes into the current logistics capacity. Starting with the labor market. A recent report from Getab states that 58 percent of Dutch drivers have considered resigning in the past year.

metadata.source_title: Collaboration is a pure necessity to expand our logistics position

text: But not without a fight. We are reaching limits when integrating additional volumes into the current logistics capacity. Starting with the labor market. A recent report from Getab states that 58 percent of Dutch drivers have considered resigning in the past year. According to Statistics Netherlands, the logistics sector is in the top 3 of industries with the most job changes: 5.5 percent in the second quarter of 2025.

metadata.source_title: Collaboration is a pure necessity to expand our logistics position

text: We are reaching limits when integrating additional volumes into the current logistics capacity. Starting with the labor market. A recent report from Getab states that 58 percent of Dutch drivers have considered resigning in the past year. According to Statistics Netherlands, the logistics sector is in the top 3 of industries with the most job changes: 5.5 percent in the second quarter of 2025. If only a few percent of drivers of the 58 percent mentioned actually resign, this will pose an acute problem for many transport chains.

metadata.source_title: Collaboration is a pure necessity to expand our logistics position

text: Starting with the labor market. A recent report from Getab states that 58 percent of Dutch drivers have considered resigning in the past year. According to Statistics Netherlands, the logistics sector is in the top 3 of industries with the most job changes: 5.5 percent in the second quarter of 2025. If only a few percent of drivers of the 58 percent mentioned actually resign, this will pose an acute problem for many transport chains. In addition, the ports suffer from congestion and part of the other domestic infrastructure is very outdated.

metadata.source_title: Collaboration is a pure necessity to expand our logistics position

text: A recent report from Getab states that 58 percent of Dutch drivers have considered resigning in the past year. According to Statistics Netherlands, the logistics sector is in the top 3 of industries with the most job changes: 5.5 percent in the second quarter of 2025. If only a few percent of drivers of the 58 percent mentioned actually resign, this will pose an acute problem for many transport chains. In addition, the ports suffer from congestion and part of the other domestic infrastructure is very outdated. Finally, transport prices have risen explosively and are continuing to rise. > “If only a few percent of drivers of the 58 percent mentioned actually resign, this will pose an acute problem for many transport chains.”.

metadata.source_title: Collaboration is a pure necessity to expand our logistics position

If only a few percent of drivers of the 58 percent mentioned actually resign, this will pose an acute problem for many transport chains. In addition, the ports suffer from congestion and part of the other domestic infrastructure is very outdated. Finally, transport prices have risen explosively and are continuing to rise. > “If only a few percent of drivers of the 58 percent mentioned actually resign, this will pose an acute problem for many transport chains.”. Bart Banning.

Decisive policy and cooperation are needed. Solving these problems requires decisive policy from all stakeholders, aiming for a future-proof sector, focused on growth opportunities. That means being ready for expected and resistant to unexpected changes.

metadata.source_title: Collaboration is a pure necessity to expand our logistics position

text:
Bart Banning. Sector Banker Transport & Logistics
Decisive policy and cooperation are needed. Solving these problems requires decisive policy from all stakeholders, aiming for a future-proof sector, focused on growth opportunities. That means being ready for expected and resistant to unexpected changes. As one team, from one mission. > “No logistics without infrastructure, it's that simple.”.

text: Bart Banning. Sector Banker Transport & Logistics. The first stakeholder that must be on alert is the Ministry of Infrastructure and Water Management. Investments in road, water and railways are crucial to make optimal use of all transport modalities. And the ministry must respond to the consequences of climate change, such as periods of low water on inland waterways.

metadata.source_title: Collaboration is a pure necessity to expand our logistics position

text: Sector Banker Transport & Logistics. The first stakeholder that must be on alert is the Ministry of Infrastructure and Water Management. Investments in road, water and railways are crucial to make optimal use of all transport modalities. And the ministry must respond to the consequences of climate change, such as periods of low water on inland waterways. Major maintenance and expansion of the infrastructure is planned in the coming years.

metadata.source_title: Collaboration is a pure necessity to expand our logistics position

text: The first stakeholder that must be on alert is the Ministry of Infrastructure and Water Management. Investments in road, water and railways are crucial to make optimal use of all transport modalities. And the ministry must respond to the consequences of climate change, such as periods of low water on inland waterways. Major maintenance and expansion of the infrastructure is planned in the coming years. It is crucial that the next government also bears this in mind.

Without infrastructure there is no logistics, it's as simple as that.

metadata.source_title: Collaboration is a pure necessity to expand our logistics position

text: It is crucial that the next government also bears this in mind. Without infrastructure there is no logistics, it's as simple as that. Secondly, an appeal to the participants of the Logistics Alliance. The ambitions of the modal shift have not been realized. The July 2025 report from the Expertise and Innovation Center for Inland Shipping and Royal Inland Shipping of the Netherlands is clear: road transport in Europe has gained market share over the past ten years at the expense of inland shipping and rail transport.

metadata.source_title: Collaboration is a pure necessity to expand our logistics position

text: Without infrastructure there is no logistics, it's as simple as that. Secondly, an appeal to the participants of the Logistics Alliance. The ambitions of the modal shift have not been realized. The July 2025 report from the Expertise and Innovation Center for Inland Shipping and Royal Inland Shipping of the Netherlands is clear: road transport in Europe has gained market share over the past ten years at the expense of inland shipping and rail transport. Renewed commitment to the modal shift is necessary to make better use of the capacity on the railways and our waterways.

metadata.source_title: Collaboration is a pure necessity to expand our logistics position

text: Secondly, an appeal to the participants of the Logistics Alliance. The ambitions of the modal shift have not been realized. The July 2025 report from the Expertise and Innovation Center for Inland Shipping and Royal Inland Shipping of the Netherlands is clear: road transport in Europe has gained market share over the past ten years at the expense of inland shipping and rail transport. Renewed commitment to the modal shift is necessary to make better use of the capacity on the railways and our waterways. This reduces emissions and offers a solution for staff shortages and traffic jams in road transport. > “The only path to structural cost control is higher productivity.”.

After a difficult year, in which international trade declined due to a rapid reduction of excess inventories, the transport sector has started a cautious recovery. metadata.source_title: Collaboration is a pure necessity to expand our logistics position

text:
After a difficult year, in which international trade declined due to a rapid reduction of excess inventories, the transport sector has started a cautious recovery. At the beginning of 2024, freight volumes increased in both container shipping and aviation. Thanks to the improving economic growth, the sector can recover further. However, the recovery is being held back by lower demand for building materials. In the meantime, road transport will have to deal with a pile-up of environmental regulations in the coming years. Thanks to the improving economic growth, the sector can recover further. However, the recovery is being held back by lower demand for building materials. In the meantime, road transport will have to deal with a pile-up of environmental regulations in the coming years.
